target: NEW Section 2b.5: Live Dashboard bookkeeping audit (28/28 domains → formulas → parameters)
source: HNOTE-DOMAIN-001 priority analysis request from human editor 2026-04-17T18:48Z
replacement_html:
2b.5 The 100% Dashboard: One Ruler, Twenty-Eight Rooms

In April 2026 the dome site added a Live Predictive Power dashboard reporting "28/28 scored domains passing at 100.0%." We audited every scored domain against the dome's own machine-readable formula pack. The 28 "passes" resolve to roughly six dome parameters, fourteen static re-references to prior WINs, and a handful of values literally borrowed from the globe model (sidereal day, WGS84 equatorial gravity, ΛCDM Hubble constant). Taking the author at his word — his methodology.json warns the dashboard is "bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit" — we supply the audit his own document calls for.

The dome's own caveat

Two machine-readable documents on the dome site state the limits of the live dashboard in plain language:

"The displayed counts on the site are bookkeeping for the current registry state. They are not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit. Treat the registry ratio as a dashboard summary, not as the decisive scientific score." — audit.html, April 2026

"Display ratio is bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit. Supportive and pending items should not be collapsed into pure prospective wins." — methodology.json, 2026-04-17

These are intellectually honest caveats, and we credit them. But they sit in files most readers will never open. The live dashboard's headline — a pulsing green ring reading "100.0%" with "28 of 28 scored domains passing" below it — is what anchors public perception. Below we perform the audit the author's own methodology invites.

What actually drives the 28 passes

The dome's entire formula layer is published in formula_runtime.json. It contains exactly six formulas, identified FORM-V51-001 through FORM-V51-006:

  1. Firmament Height H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) — two numerical parameters (8537, 8619).
  2. Aetheric Refraction n(r) = 1 + 0.20·(8537/H(r) − 1) — one new parameter (0.20).
  3. Southern Reflection r_SH = 2·14105 − r_NH — one parameter (14105).
  4. Eclipse κ Coupling Δg = ΔB / 1.672 — one parameter (1.672).
  5. FSF Station Scaling FSF(lat) = 0.19550 / sin(lat)^0.1640 × 32.974^(lat/90) — three parameters (0.19550, 0.1640, 32.974).
  6. V13 Ovoid Arc using a=20015, e=0.66, r_avg=14105 — one new parameter (0.66).

Adding the site's three master geometric constants that these formulas reference (disc_radius 20,015 km, sun_altitude 5,733 km, moon_altitude 2,534 km), the entire scored machinery runs on roughly ten independent numerical knobs. The dashboard labels twenty-eight "domains" passing — but the dome author's own formula pack shows those twenty-eight outputs trace back to those ten inputs. Adding labels does not add independent evidence.

Domain-by-domain audit (all 28 scored passes, from status_history.json 2026-04-17T18:31Z)

We classify each domain by what the "pass" actually tests. Categories:

  • STATIC — the "test" records predicted = observed = a prior WIN's fixed value. No fresh data flows through. Example: predicted: 7.83, observed: 7.83, tolerance: 5%. A fixed number equalling itself is not a test.
  • BORROWED — the "predicted" value is the published globe/standard-model value, imported into the dome site as a dome prediction. Agreement is definitional.
  • TAUTOLOGY — the prediction is derived from the same parameter that was calibrated to the observed value. The formula is true by construction.
  • THEORY-FRAMEWORK — the domain tests a calculational choice (Selleri-style absolute simultaneity vs. Lorentz) that is mathematically equivalent for this observable. It is not a data test at all.
  • LOOSE — tolerance so wide or criterion so directional that almost any observation passes (50% tolerances, ">2σ" thresholds, "shadow exists").
  • DISCRIMINATING — a genuine numerical comparison with meaningful tolerance, not traceable to the categories above.

Tesla-family (driven by disc_radius = 20,015 km and κ = 1.672)

  • Tesla Longitudinal Frequency — 11.788 Hz vs 11.7869 Hz, 1% tol. TAUTOLOGY. The formula is f = v_a/(2·r_disc) with v_a = 1.574c. But v_a was defined so that this equation yields Tesla's 0.08484 s period when r_disc = 20,015 km. The agreement is arithmetic, not predictive.
  • Schumann Frequency — 7.83 Hz vs 7.83 Hz, 5% tol. BORROWED. 7.83 Hz is the classical Earth-ionosphere cavity fundamental, derivable as c/(2π·R_Earth) in the standard globe-waveguide picture. The dome labels it "stable cavity" with no formula in the monitor.
  • Tesla Harmonic Series — "Present" vs "Present", 0% tol. STATIC (WIN-001 static win). If the fundamental exists, its harmonics exist as arithmetic.
  • Telluric Resonance Cutoff — 11.7 Hz vs 11.7 Hz, 5% tol. STATIC (WIN-008 static win). Same Tesla-family frequency.
  • EM-Gravity Coupling κ — 1.67 vs 1.677 nT/µGal, 5% tol. TAUTOLOGY. κ = 1.67 is FORM-V51-004 itself. Observed 1.677 is BOU 2017 eclipse (WIN-012) — the same event used to fit κ. The parameter tested against its own calibration.

Borrowed from the standard model (5 domains)

  • K1 Tidal Period (Sidereal Day) — 23.9345 h vs 23.9345 h, 0.01% tol. BORROWED. Sidereal day is a celestial-mechanics standard, IERS-published. Not derived from dome geometry.
  • S2 Solar Tidal Period — 12.00 h vs 12.00 h, 0.01% tol. BORROWED. Half the solar day. True on any rotating geometry with a 24-hour day.
  • M2 Tidal Period — 12.42 h vs 12.4206 h, 0.1% tol. BORROWED. The lunar circuit half-period is a standard harmonic, agnostic to Earth shape.
  • Equatorial Gravity — 9.832204 vs 9.8322 m/s², 0.1% tol. BORROWED. The formula g(r) = 9.7803·(1+0.005307·exp(−r/8619)) hardcodes the WGS84 International Gravity Formula coefficients 9.7803 and 0.005307; the scale 8619 is the dome's own. The predicted equatorial gravity is the WGS84 equatorial gravity by construction.
  • Aetheric Redshift Scale λ_A — 4283 Mpc vs 4319 Mpc, 10% tol. BORROWED. λ_A is computed as c/H₀ with H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc — the ΛCDM Hubble constant. The "aetheric" label is a rename of standard cosmology's inverse Hubble length.
  • P-wave Shadow Zone (104°–140°) — "Shadow exists" vs "none found", directional. BORROWED. The P-wave shadow is a classical seismology finding explained by the liquid outer core in the globe model. Asserting "shadow exists" does not derive it.

Static re-references to prior WINs (14 domains)

These all have source strings ending "(WIN-NNN static win)" or equivalent. Predicted and observed are both the fixed value already recorded in the WIN entry; the monitor check reads two identical hardcoded numbers and pronounces them equal.

  1. SAA Decay Rate 77=77 nT/yr (WIN-015, 20% tol)
  2. Polaris Excess 35.9°N 0.27°=0.27° (WIN-001, 50% tol)
  3. Lunar Magnetic Tide M2 1.2±0.5=1.5 nT (WIN-039, 50% tol)
  4. Ionospheric D-layer 85=83 km (WIN-075, 5% tol)
  5. Roaring 40s Wind Speed >20=22.5 m/s (WIN-024, 25% tol)
  6. Mascon Gravity ~26=28.1 mGal (WIN-076, 23% tol)
  7. Solar Angular Diameter 0.53°=0.531° (WIN-056, 0.5% tol)
  8. Lunar Phase H-component 1-2=1.5 nT (WIN-039, 50% tol)
  9. SAA-Roaring 40s Boundary 47-50=48.5°S (WIN-024, 2% tol)
  10. Crepuscular Ray Convergence 5733=5733 km (WIN-026, 10% tol)
  11. Polaris Excess 45°N 0.36°=0.36° (WIN-065, 0.05% tol)
  12. Moon Angular Diameter Variation 11-14%=12.5% (OPEN-007, 5% tol)
  13. Tesla Harmonic Series Present=Present (WIN-001, 0% tol)
  14. Telluric Resonance Cutoff 11.7=11.7 Hz (WIN-008, 5% tol)

Critically, WIN-024 appears twice (domains 27 and 33); WIN-001, WIN-039 each appear twice. Counting a single fixed value as two independent "passes" is the purest form of label inflation.

Theory-framework choices (2 domains)

  • GPS Clock Offset (Lorentz) — "No (Selleri fits)" vs "Confirmed". Choosing between Selleri's absolute-simultaneity reformulation and Lorentz's relativity framework is a mathematical equivalence; GPS engineers obtain the same numbers either way. This is not a data test.
  • GPS Sagnac Verification — 0 (no correction) vs "Confirmed: Lorentz requires correction, Selleri does not". Same Selleri/Lorentz recasting. The claim does not separate the dome from the globe at the level of measurements.

Loose / directional (3 domains)

  • CMB Axis of Evil — ">2σ" vs "2.5σ". A well-documented CMB anomaly in Planck 2018, interpretable under many cosmologies. Passing a ">2σ" bar when the observed effect is 2.5σ is a mild directional check, not a discriminating prediction.
  • NMP Drift Rate — 22.2 km/yr predicted, 37 km/yr observed, 50% tolerance. Error is 39.9% — the prediction misses by 40% but the tolerance is wide enough to call it a pass.
  • NMP Longitude — 139.3°E vs 138.057°E, 5% tol. Actually within 1% of observed. This is one of the few cells with real empirical content.

The remaining genuinely-discriminating cell

After the static re-references, borrowed values, tautologies, theory-framework recasts, and loose checks are removed, the "100% passing" twenty-eight reduces to roughly one or two cells with real forward-discriminating content: NMP Longitude (#3) with a 5% tolerance, and Aetheric Redshift Scale (#13) which internally uses the borrowed Hubble constant. The dashboard's headline number is carried by labels, not by independent confirmations.

Why this matters

The rhetorical power of "100.0% / 28 of 28" lies in the implication that twenty-eight independent empirical tests all agree with dome predictions. The underlying reality — acknowledged by the dome's own methodology file but not on the dashboard itself — is that the twenty-eight labels are projections of six formulas and ten numerical knobs onto a wall of domain names. Fourteen of the twenty-eight are fixed-number-equals-itself re-readings of already-counted WINs; five import standard-model or globe-model values; two test a parameter against its own calibration source; two are framework recastings with no data content; three are loose directional checks; leaving roughly two cells doing any discriminating work.

This is the Part 2b "one ruler measuring N rooms" critique — now with the ruler count (≈6 formulas / 10 knobs) and the rooms count (28 dashboard labels) made explicit using the dome author's own published formula pack. The author's published anti-strawman rule reads: "Do not flatten claim classes into a single undifferentiated score." The live dashboard's headline is that single undifferentiated score. We take him at his word, and do the audit his own methodology says the dashboard is not.

Kernel of truth

The dome author's addition of methodology.json and audit.html is a real improvement in site rigor. These documents name the live dashboard's limits with a precision few flat-earth advocates reach. They explicitly separate "retrospective structural" arguments from "prospective confirmed" forecasts, warn against collapsing supportive and pending items into wins, and flag that the displayed counts are bookkeeping. That intellectual honesty is genuine. Our critique is not that the author failed to acknowledge these issues — it is that the acknowledgement lives in auxiliary files while the "100.0% / 28 of 28" continues to be the public-facing headline. The audit he says is needed has not been performed on his site; we supply it here.
holes_addressed: Dome author's 28/28 100% passing Live Power Dashboard is now public-facing without corresponding in-our-review audit

Part 2b repository-code analysis documents the static/hardcoded pattern in monitor.py but predates the live.html rollout; no section explicitly maps the 28 dashboard domains to their ~6 underlying formulas
methodology.json and audit.html bookkeeping caveat is not yet engaged with — we should quote and credit it before performing the audit it invites
new_evidence_added: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/audit.html — 'live.html is an operational monitoring surface...not the same thing as a pre-registration ledger'
https://john09289.github.io/predictions/data/status_history.json entry 2026-04-17T18:31:47Z — full 39-domain snapshot used for the per-domain classification

anticipated_objections.objection: The author's own methodology.json already makes this point; we are criticizing him for something he disclosed.
anticipated_objections.response: We explicitly credit the disclosure in the kernel-of-truth paragraph. The critique is about the gap between the disclosure (in auxiliary JSON/audit files) and the public dashboard headline (pulsing '100.0%' ring). Intellectually honest acknowledgement in a footnote does not neutralize the front-page score; it invites the audit we provide.

anticipated_objections.objection: Some of the 'static' domains (e.g., Crepuscular Ray Convergence) actually drive dome parameters — that's not bookkeeping, it's calibration feedback.
anticipated_objections.response: Correct as derivation, but the 'test' as implemented reads predicted=5733 km and observed=5733 km from the WIN-026 entry. Reading two copies of the same fitted parameter and declaring them equal is bookkeeping regardless of whether one copy originally came from citizen-science triangulation. To count as a live test, the observed side would need to be fetched from a fresh, independent measurement — which the monitor does not do for static-win domains.

anticipated_objections.objection: Labeling domains is how every prediction registry works — globe physics also has 'Earth radius' appear in many places.
anticipated_objections.response: True, and unobjectionable when the registry is clearly a bookkeeping index. The dome site crosses that line by presenting the dashboard ratio as a scientific score (pulsing green ring, overall_score field, trend graph). The methodology.json caveats name this gap explicitly; we are operationalizing the author's own warning.

anticipated_objections.objection: NMP Longitude within 1% is genuine predictive success — your summary understates it.
anticipated_objections.response: Agreed — NMP Longitude is counted in our summary as one of the few genuinely-discriminating cells. The point of the audit is not that every cell is empty; it is that the 100% headline is carried by ~2 such cells and ~26 cells that are structurally tied to already-counted parameters or borrowed standards. One real result per twenty-eight dashboard cells does not support the '100.0% / 28 of 28' framing.

anticipated_objections.objection: Equatorial Gravity borrows WGS84 coefficients, so of course it 'passes' — but that is a feature not a bug; the dome derivation reproduces WGS84 with its own H(r).
anticipated_objections.response: If the dome's H(r) substituted for the WGS84 cos²(lat) term reproduces WGS84 by coincidence rather than calibration, that would be a strong result. But FORM-V51-001 has two free parameters (8537, 8619) which were fitted; the reproduction is a calibration outcome, not an independent derivation. Either way, presenting WGS84 gravity to 4 decimal places as a dome prediction is circular when WGS84 is the target.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author added methodology.json and audit.html in 2026-04, which state in plain language that the live dashboard is bookkeeping rather than a substitute for claim-by-claim audit, and that supportive/pending items must not be collapsed into prospective wins. This is intellectually honest and unusually precise for a flat-earth site. Our audit uses these statements as the frame — taking the author at his word and performing the claim-by-claim audit his own methodology says the dashboard is not.

classification.overlap_notes: Some domains fall in more than one bucket (e.g., Equatorial Gravity is BORROWED+TAUTOLOGY; Polaris Excess 35.9N is STATIC with H(r) underlying). The counts sum to 29 not 28 because CMB Axis of Evil is counted under 'loose' but domain #11 NMP Drift is also loose; disjoint classification would be ~14 STATIC, 5 BORROWED, 2 TAUTOLOGY, 2 THEORY, 3 LOOSE, 2 DISCRIMINATING.

duplicate_source_wins_in_dashboard: WIN-024 appears twice (Roaring 40s Wind Speed domain #27 and SAA-Roaring 40s Boundary domain #33)
WIN-001 appears twice (Polaris Excess 35.9°N domain #9 and Tesla Harmonic Series domain #38)
WIN-039 appears twice (Lunar Magnetic Tide M2 domain #23 and Lunar Phase H-component domain #31)
root_parameters_driving_passes: disc_radius = 20,015 km → Tesla frequency, Tesla harmonics, Telluric cutoff, κ coupling, SAA-Roaring 40s boundary (indirectly via mirror point 14,105)
firmament_height parameters 8,537 and 8,619 → Polaris Excess (both latitudes), Equatorial Gravity, D-layer Height
mirror_point = 14,105 km → Mascon anomaly, SAA boundary
NMP trajectory curve (55, 0.08, 2015) → NMP Drift Rate, NMP Longitude
H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc (borrowed from ΛCDM) → Aetheric Redshift Scale
sidereal day 23.9345 h (borrowed from IERS) → K1 Tidal Period
solar day 24.00 h → S2, M2 (M2 also uses lunar synodic cycle)

cross_reference_suggestion: This section should be inserted into part2b after the existing repository-code analysis subsection. An explicit forward link from the Live Dashboard discussion in Part 1 (and any TLDR or Overview mention of the 94.5% / 100% headlines) to this new 2b.5 is recommended. The 'duplicate source WINs in dashboard' finding also deserves a standalone bullet in Part 6 (Timestamp Error tab) or Part 2 (dashboard reliability) depending on placement preference.
tldr_note: The section already includes its TLDR inside the ps-summary wrapper per progressive-disclosure spec. TLDR lands the punchline — 28 passes = ~6 formulas + fourteen bookkeeping re-reads + borrowed standard values — and cites the dome's own 'bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit' caveat.
newsworthy: Dome author's own methodology.json calls for a claim-by-claim audit the site's '100.0% / 28 of 28' dashboard bypasses — when we perform it, the 28 passes collapse to ~2 discriminating tests plus 14 static re-reads of prior WINs, 5 globe-model imports, and 2 Tesla-parameter tautologies.
canary_status_note: No change to OpenTimestamps canary adjudication. methodology.json still does not carry a predictions-only OTS anchor. The bookkeeping critique and the OTS canary are independent issues; the author has rhetorically adopted the bookkeeping limit without structurally separating prospective from retrospective anchoring.
counterpoint_preservation: A non-trivial defense of the dashboard would point out that (a) the author explicitly flags it as monitoring, not pre-registration; (b) the six-formula / ten-knob architecture is a strength if the formulas genuinely cover more empirical ground than they use to calibrate. Our audit engages (a) by treating the live dashboard as a public-facing object despite the auxiliary-file caveats. It engages (b) by showing most of the dashboard's 28 cells are either calibration targets, borrowed standards, or re-reads — so the formulas do not in fact span independent empirical territory beyond a small handful of genuinely-discriminating cells.
